content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to design a special stack that not only supports the usual stack operations but also allows retrieving the **minimum element** instantly.

To achieve this, Chef needs a stack implementation that can perform all operations in constant time **O(1)O(1)O(1)**.

The stack should efficiently handle a large number of operations without delay.

Class DescriptionChef will implement a class named **minimumStackminimumStackminimumStack**.

The class supports the following operations:



**addValue(val)addValue(val)addValue(val)**

Adds the integer **valvalval** onto the stack.




**removeTopValue()removeTopValue()removeTopValue()**

Removes the top element from the stack.




**getTopValue()getTopValue()getTopValue()**

Returns the top element of the stack.




**getMinimumValue()getMinimumValue()getMinimumValue()**

Returns the minimum element currently present in the stack.
constraints: All pop, top and getMin operations are performed on a non-empty stack
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer Q, the number of operations. The next Q lines each contain an operation. Operations are one of: push x (adds integer x to the stack), pop (removes the top element), top (returns the current top element), getMin (returns the current minimum element)
content.outputFormat: For every operation that returns a value (top, getMin) output the integer on its own line. For operations that do not return a value (push, pop) output the word "None" on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case pushes -2,0,-3. Minimum becomes -3. getMin prints -3. pop removes -3, minimum reverts to -2. top prints 0. getMin prints -2. Second test case pushes -3 and getMin prints -3.
Push 5,10,3. Minimum is 3. getMin outputs 3. pop removes 3, new minimum is 5.
After pushing 10 and 20, minimum stays 10, which is printed.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use two stacks: one for storing the actual elements and another for storing the minimum elements seen so far. When we push an element onto the stack, we also push it onto the minimum stack if it is less than or equal to the current minimum. When we pop an element from the stack, we also pop it from the minimum stack if it is equal to the current minimum. This way, we can always get the minimum element in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q) per test case, O(T*Q) total. The space complexity is O(Q) as we need to store all the elements in the stack.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(Q) — the dominant structure is the stack.
